assumptions: The developer has a supported desktop OS, browser, and email address.
No Postman desktop install, Postman account, collection, or saved Echo request exists yet.

developer_goal: Install Postman, create an account, send the Postman Echo request from the official Quick start, save it, and review a status-code test result.

first_success.outcome: The Quick start request is saved and Test Results show the Status code is 200 assertion for the Postman Echo response.
first_success.observable_signal: After the final Send, the Response pane is populated and Test Results shows the status-code test result.

route_selection.surface: Postman desktop Quick start with Postman Echo
route_selection.rule: Use the official Quick start through save and test only after resolving whether first success stops at the first Echo response in the lightweight client.

candidates.name: Desktop Quick start through collection save and Test Results
candidates.reason: Matches the current Quick start document end state, but requires sign-in before save and adds personalize-your-experience fields whose exact requiredness versus social signup is not fully settled.

candidates.name: Unsigned lightweight API Client first Echo Send only
candidates.reason: Docs say the desktop app can send requests before sign-in; stopping after the first response is shorter but omits the Quick start's save and test sections.

candidates.name: Email signup versus GitHub, Google, or SSO
candidates.reason: Official sign-up docs present peer identity methods with different verification and redirect steps.

required_path.action: Download, install, and open the Postman desktop app.
required_path.observable_result: The Postman desktop workbench is open.

required_path.action: Open a new request tab, enter the Postman Echo GET URL, and select Send.
required_path.observable_result: The Response pane shows the Echo response body.

required_path.action: Create a Postman account and sign in before saving the request.

required_fields.label: Email
required_fields.notes: Documented for the email signup option.

required_path.observable_result: The developer is signed in and can save requests to collections.

required_path.action: Save the request into a new named collection.
required_path.observable_result: The request appears under Collections in the sidebar.

required_path.action: Add the Status code: Code is 200 post-response snippet, Send again, and open Test Results.
required_path.observable_result: Test Results shows the status-code assertion outcome for the Echo response.

prerequisites.description: Ability to install the Postman desktop app on the developer's machine.

external_gates.description: Email verification is required for the email signup option before the account can be used.

unavoidable_waits.description: Postman must deliver the 6-digit email verification code for the email signup route.

platform_outcomes.description: Postman Echo returns a response that the desktop client renders in the Response pane.
Postman runs the post-response test and populates Test Results.

excluded.item: Connecting a local Git repository during signup
excluded.reason: Official signup docs label the Git connection step optional.
Presented as an optional preference during signup.

excluded.item: Reading broader Postman Learning Center overview pages
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a product action.

sources.title: Postman quick start
evidence_supported: Status code test and Test Results

sources.title: Get started overview
evidence_supported: Quick start positioning for new users

sources.title: Sign up for Postman
evidence_supported: Email, username, password, and verification code

uncertainties.question: Does first success stop after the first unsigned Echo response, or must the path include official Quick start save and test with account creation?
uncertainties.impact: Stopping early removes account fields and collection/test actions; continuing requires choosing an identity method and counting personalization fields.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus rule choice between lightweight first response and full Quick start end state, plus a fresh desktop signup walkthrough for the chosen identity method.
